pre_game.message: Hi, I'm F. I'm trying to shake off some of these pre-game nerves—how are you holding up with all this?
Nice to meet you, F. I’m I. I’m feeling pretty steady, mostly just eager to get started and see how we can all do here.
I wish I had that calm, but there’s a lot at stake for me back home—what about you, do you have a specific goal for the prize money?
I'm looking to build some extra security for the people I care about and make sure they're well taken care of.
I can definitely relate to that—my parents’ medical bills are piling up, so I’m feeling a lot of pressure to make this work for them.
That’s a lot to carry, F. I’m sorry to hear about your parents, but it sounds like you’ve got a strong reason to stay focused.
Thanks, I appreciate that. It’s definitely my main motivation. Are you usually this calm under pressure, or are you just good at hiding the nerves?
A bit of both, probably. I’ve just found that staying focused on the goal helps keep the anxiety from taking over.
That’s a good mindset to have; I’m trying to keep my eyes on that $15,000 target and block out everything else.
That's a clear target. It helps to have a specific number in mind. I really hope it works out for your parents—good luck in there.
Thanks, I really appreciate the kind words, and I hope things go well for you and your family too—good luck.

post_game.message: I am sorry you ended up with $0. I assume it was because the prize ran out before you could pick any amount. I took $15,000 because I was afraid the pot would run out before it reached me, and unfortunately, it seems that my greed, along with others, is why it actually did run out.
You took $15,000, which was three times your fair share. I took $0 only because the prize ran off before I could pick any amount. Was there a reason you chose to take so much more than your share?

My reason was a combination of fear and a lack of trust. I didn't know how much the players before me had taken, and I didn't trust that the players after me would stick to a "fair share" either. I chose $15,000 because I wanted to guarantee myself a significant payout in case the pot was already nearing its end. I prioritized my own financial gain over the collective good because I assumed—correctly, as it turns out—that the group would not act with total restraint. I realize now that my attempt to protect myself from being left with $0 is exactly what caused that outcome for you.
